changes: ios/Upload/Sources/PickleballUpload/RenderGatewayClient.swift:105 adds partial status, missing-capability decoding, lossless trust/provenance JSON, authenticated artifact fetching, and manifest resolution.
ios/Upload/Sources/PickleballUpload/UploadQueue.swift:13 persists the identity chain; :136 fail-closes row availability; :332 polls and validates the matching job.
ios/App/DinkVisionModels.swift:500 distinguishes real captures from explicit samples; :521 adds typed replay routing.
ios/App/DinkVisionRuntimeConfiguration.swift:76 marks only the seeded row as sample; :99 wires authenticated job polling.
ios/App/DinkVisionUploadCoordinator.swift:188 exposes honest partial, ready, and processing labels.
ios/App/AppRootView.swift:1289 routes the selected row; :1641 loads its route; :1710 confines bundled-fixture loading to the explicit sample case.
ios/Replay/Sources/PickleballReplay/World/WorldViewerManifest.swift:8 mirrors the web URL schema, including ball_arc_render_url.
ios/Replay/Sources/PickleballReplay/World/WorldBundle.swift:57 adds file loading; :86 authenticated async loading; :133 correct HTTP/file resolution and explicit optional-asset issues.
ios/Upload/Tests/PickleballUploadTests/UploadManifestTests.swift:220 covers partial/trust/provenance; :241 covers the URLProtocol gateway.
ios/Upload/Tests/PickleballUploadTests/UploadQueueTests.swift:248 proves row identity persistence; :300 proves typed not-ready state.
ios/Replay/Tests/PickleballReplayTests/WorldBundleTests.swift:45, :67, and :113 cover file/HTTP resolution, optional failures, no substitution, and schema parity.
ios/AppTests/DinkVisionStateTests.swift:251 and :295 prove real rows never route to fixtures and only the explicit sample does.

full_suite.failure_notes: ReplayPersonBenchmarkRunnerTests.testDetectEveryTwoFramesCountsOnlyDetectorInvocationsAsProcessed skipped because host AVAssetReader cannot decode the bundled synthetic video. Plain swift test was initially blocked by the unwritable ~/.cache/clang; /tmp caches plus --disable-sandbox produced the green full suite. xcodebuild was separately attempted and blocked by sandbox-exec/CoreSimulatorService.

honest_issues: xcodebuild build-for-testing is not green. Package resolution failed with `sandbox-exec: sandbox_apply: Operation not permitted`; CoreSimulatorService was also unavailable. Manual iOS package build and app/test typechecks passed but do not replace xcodebuild.

NS-01.2b remains owner-blocked. Only the signed owner device with production authentication can prove record/Photos import -> upload -> GPU job -> persisted matching manifest -> authenticated on-device rendering. No physical-device claim was made.
The single SwiftPM skip is a host-codec limitation, not a pass for that benchmark.
VERIFIED=0 remains binding; this is a scoped native routing pass.
next: Manager should run simulator build-for-testing outside this sandbox, then perform and save the signed physical NS-01.2b capture/job/manifest/replay trace.
